title: gh-142961: Fix constant folding len(tuple) in JIT
author: Fidget-Spinner
body:
The optimization broke and causes segfaults now, but there were no tests so no one noticed. I added a test so we will catch it in the future.

<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-142961 -->
* Issue: gh-142961
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: @corona10 I think this was introduced in the `_CALL_TUPLE_1` refcount elimination work :(. Not your fault though, there were no tests for this optimization it seems!

comments.author: markshannon
comments.body:
Actually, let's not pop the `callable`, just declare it dead.
This is simpler and has less shuffling:
```
op(_CALL_LEN, (len_func, null, arg -- res, a)) {

 macro(CALL_LEN) =
      unused/1 +
      unused/2 +
      _GUARD_NOS_NULL +
      _GUARD_CALLABLE_LEN +
      _CALL_LEN +
      POP_TOP;
```

comments.author: Fidget-Spinner
comments.body: @markshannon `len` is not immortal, only on FT builds it is. We need to decref it otherwise there will be refleaks.
